title: Clausena harmandiana
intro: Clausena harmandiana, also known as Thai plume or Thai orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Clausena harmandiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Clausena harmandiana is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its health benefits, and it is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
taxonomy: Clausena harmandiana is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is part of the larger order Sapindales, which also includes other families such as the Sapindaceae and the Anacardiaceae. Clausena harmandiana is further classified as a member of the genus Clausena, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. The exact classification of Clausena harmandiana is Clausena harmandiana (Perr.) Swingle, which is a species of flowering plant. The classification of this plant is an important part of its identification and understanding of its relationships with other plants.
